Tests the behavior of the modulus operation with Infinity and zero in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an `assert` statement to verify the result of a mathematical operation. It checks if the result of the modulus operation `Infinity % 0` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The `assert` function is used to ensure that the expression evaluates to `true`, confirming that the operation behaves as expected according to JavaScript's arithmetic rules. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case in mathematical operations.
